title: How to Fight an Economic War: China's Parallel Financial System
content:
In recent years, global economic competition has increasingly taken the form of strategic economic warfare. One of the primary battlegrounds is the financial infrastructure, where China has been actively developing a parallel system to reduce its reliance on Western intermediaries. By launching the Cross-Border Interbank Payment System (CIPS) and advancing digital currency initiatives, Beijing seeks to build a resilient financial network that could scale up quickly in a crisis. While these efforts do not immediately threaten dollar dominance, they serve as a crucial insurance policy against potential financial sanctions. This proactive approach underscores the shifting dynamics of global financial power.

The weaponization of supply chain chokepoints has further intensified this economic conflict. For example, when the United States imposed tariffs on Chinese goods, Beijing retaliated by restricting the export of vital rare-earth elements. This move quickly strained American manufacturing, highlighting vulnerabilities in sectors ranging from automotive to defense. Similarly, strategic dependencies on clean energy technologies and essential components like batteries have given foreign powers significant leverage. To safeguard economic security, nations must move beyond scattershot onshoring policies and focus on mitigating specific supply chain vulnerabilities through collective action and targeted investments.
